title: frontend: EditorDialog: Warn when resource modified during edit
author: sudhidutta7694

findings.description: ResourceWatcher.useEffect suppresses exhaustive-deps via eslint-disable comment. Missing deps: onClose, handleSave, setErrorMessage, activityId. Project convention requires fixing (wrap handleClose and handleSave in useCallback, add all to deps array) rather than suppressing.

No Vitest tests for the new ResourceWatcher component or the updated conflict-detection flow in EditorDialog.tsx. A unit test verifying watchedItem changes call Activity.update with the correct item prop would guard against regressions.
Inline onEditorChanged arrow function () => setErrorMessage('') inside Activity.update JSX creates a new reference on each watch event. Minor: hoist or wrap in useCallback for clarity.
ResourceWatcher only mounts when isEditorOpen=true, preventing unnecessary WebSocket connections when the pencil button is rendered in list views or Storybook.
store.getState() guard in ResourceWatcher.useEffect correctly avoids the isActivityOpenRef reset-on-remount bug described in the PR. Direct store access is justified in this non-render context.
handleClose sets isEditorOpen=false before Activity.close, correctly unmounting ResourceWatcher and tearing down the WebSocket watch to prevent leaks.
Both new commit subjects follow project convention: correct area:SubArea:Verb format, capitalised verb, under 72 characters.
